questions.question: When an AI agent 'uses a tool', what actually happens?
options: The AI directly connects to the internet and runs code
The AI sends a structured request to the host app, which actually executes it and sends the result back
The AI downloads and installs the tool, then runs it
The tool runs automatically whenever the AI mentions it
questions.explanation: The AI is like a smart friend who can suggest actions but can't pick up your phone. It sends a structured message saying 'I'd like to do X with these inputs', the host app does it, and sends the result back. The AI never directly executes anything.

questions.question: Skills are special plugins you install — they require a database and a server to work.
questions.explanation: False! Skills are just text files (like recipe cards) that get loaded into the AI's context when needed. No database, no server, no installation. Just a markdown file on disk that the host reads at the right time.

questions.question: MCP is best described as:
options: A programming language for AI agents
A universal standard for connecting AI agents to external services — like USB-C for AI
A type of AI model that's smarter than others
A way to train AI models on your own data
questions.explanation: MCP (Model Context Protocol) is a standard protocol — like USB-C standardized phone chargers, MCP standardizes how AI agents connect to services like GitHub, databases, and file systems. One standard instead of custom code for every integration.
